Urnula craterium.notes: Devils urn. Black urn-shaped cup. Inedible.

Leccinum albostipitatum.notes: Widely recognized as edible. Always cook thoroughly and verify with an expert.

Lactarius deliciosus.notes: Saffron milkcap. Orange cap, green staining, grows with pine. Edible.

Clitocybe nebularis.notes: Clouded funnel. Grey-brown funnel cap. Edible but some report GI issues.

Hypholoma fasciculare.notes: Sulphur tuft. Clustered on stumps, greenish gills. Poisonous.

Lactarius torminosus.notes: Woolly milkcap. Pinkish woolly cap, very hot/peppery. Poisonous.

Lycoperdon perlatum.notes: Common puffball. Round with spines, white inside when young. Edible.

Verpa bohemica.notes: Early morel. Thimble-shaped cap. Poisonous raw, risky even cooked.

Schizophyllum commune.notes: Splitgill. Small fan-shaped with split gills. Inedible.

Leccinum aurantiacum.notes: Widely recognized as edible. Always cook thoroughly and verify with an expert.

Phellinus igniarius.notes: Cramp balls. Black hard bracket. Inedible.

Suillus luteus.notes: Slippery jack. Slimy brown cap, purple pores. Edible, peel skin first.

Coltricia perennis.notes: Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume without positive identification by a certified mycologist.

Cetraria islandica.notes: Iceland moss. Fruticose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Amanita muscaria.notes: Fly agaric. Bright red cap with white spots. Contains muscimol. Poisonous.

Pholiota aurivella.notes: Golden pholiota. Scaly golden cap on wood. Edible.

Trichaptum biforme.notes: Violet-toothed polypore. Violet spines underneath. Inedible.

Artomyces pyxidatus.notes: Crown-tipped coral. White coral with crown tips. Edible.

Calocera viscosa.notes: Yellow stagshorn. Jelly fungus, antler-like. Inedible.

Sarcosoma globosum.notes: Black bulgar. Black jelly-like blobs. Inedible.

Evernia prunastri.notes: Oakmoss. Fruticose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Laetiporus sulphureus.notes: Chicken of the woods. Bright orange-yellow shelves on wood. Excellent edible.

Lobaria pulmonaria.notes: Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume without positive identification by a certified mycologist.

Bjerkandera adusta.notes: Smoky polypore. Smoky grey bracket. Inedible.

Vulpicida pinastri.notes: Powdered sunshine lichen. Yellow-green lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Imleria badia.notes: Bay bolete. Chestnut cap, yellow pores bruise blue-green. Excellent edible.

Evernia mesomorpha.notes: Boreal oakmoss. Fruticose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Physcia adscendens.notes: Hooded rosette lichen. Foliose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Coprinellus micaceus.notes: Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume without positive identification by a certified mycologist.

Armillaria borealis.notes: Honey fungus. Clustered on wood, honey-colored cap. Edible when cooked thoroughly.

Trametes ochracea.notes: Ochre bracket. Similar to turkey tail. Inedible.

Cantharellus cibarius.notes: Golden chanterelle. Bright yellow, forked gills running down stem, apricot smell. Excellent edible.

Pseudevernia furfuracea.notes: Oakmoss lichen. Fruticose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Tremella mesenterica.notes: Witchs butter. Yellow jelly fungus. Inedible.

Leccinum versipelle.notes: Widely recognized as edible. Always cook thoroughly and verify with an expert.

Mutinus ravenelii.notes: Dog stinkhorn. Reddish phallic shape. Inedible.

Pholiota squarrosa.notes: Shaggy scalycap. Scaly cap on wood. Edible.

Amanita rubescens.notes: Blusher. Pinkish cap, flesh bruises reddish. Edible when cooked thoroughly.

Amanita pantherina.notes: Panther cap. Brown cap with white warts. Contains muscimol. Poisonous.

Sarcoscypha austriaca.notes: Scarlet elfcup. Bright red cup on wood. Inedible.

Boletus edulis.notes: King bolete / porcini. Brown cap, white pores, bulbous stem. Highly prized edible.

Coprinus comatus.notes: Shaggy ink cap. Tall white shaggy cap, auto-digests into black ink. Edible when young.

Merulius tremellosus.notes: Jelly rot. Translucent jelly fungus. Inedible.

Cladonia fimbriata.notes: Trumpet lichen. Cup lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Suillus grevillei.notes: Larch bolete. Orange-yellow cap with larch. Edible.

Apioperdon pyriforme.notes: Pear-shaped puffball. Grows on wood, white inside. Edible.

Cerioporus squamosus.notes: Dryads saddle. Scaly bracket on wood. Edible when young.

Leccinum scabrum.notes: Birch bolete. Brown cap, tall stem with black scabers. Edible.

Rhytisma acerinum.notes: Tar spot. Black spots on maple leaves. Not a mushroom — fungus on leaves.

Hypholoma lateritium.notes: Brick cap. Reddish cap, clustered on wood. Poisonous.

Flammulina velutipes.notes: Velvet shank. Dark velvety stem, orange cap. Edible.

Tricholomopsis rutilans.notes: Plums and custard. Purple-brown scaly cap on conifer wood. Inedible.

Coprinopsis atramentaria.notes: Common ink cap. Grey-brown cap. Poisonous with alcohol (disulfiram reaction).

Trametes versicolor.notes: Turkey tail. Colorful bracket with concentric zones. Inedible (too tough).

Graphis scripta.notes: Script lichen. Script-like marks on bark. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Ganoderma applanatum.notes: Artists conk. Large white bracket. Inedible.

Phellinus tremulae.notes: Aspen bracket. Hard bracket on aspen. Inedible.

Peltigera aphthosa.notes: Green dog lichen. Foliose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Parmelia sulcata.notes: Hammered shield lichen. Grey-green foliose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Fomitopsis betulina.notes: Birch polypore. White bracket on birch. Inedible.

Pleurotus pulmonarius.notes: Phoenix oyster. Pale, shell-like cap on wood. Edible.

Daedaleopsis confragosa.notes: Thin-walled maze polypore. Bracket with maze-like pores. Inedible.

Hericium coralloides.notes: Coral tooth. White coral-like fruit body on hardwood. Edible.

Trametes hirsuta.notes: Hairy bracket. White to grey bracket. Inedible.

Coprinellus disseminatus.notes: Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume without positive identification by a certified mycologist.

Kuehneromyces mutabilis.notes: Sheathed woodtuft. Two-toned cap. Poisonous — confused with honey fungus.

Pleurotus ostreatus.notes: Oyster mushroom. Shelf-like on wood, decurrent gills. Edible and cultivated.

Phlebia radiata.notes: Crimped gill. Wrinkled orange crust. Inedible.

Boletus reticulatus.notes: Summer bolete. Net-like pattern on stem. Excellent edible.

Phallus impudicus.notes: Stinkhorn. Phallic shape, foul smell. Inedible (egg stage eaten in some cultures).

Macrolepiota procera.notes: Parasol mushroom. Tall with scaly cap and snakeskin-patterned stem. Excellent edible.

Suillus granulatus.notes: Granulated bolete. No ring, milky droplets on pores. Edible.

Gyromitra esculenta.notes: False morel. Brain-like, reddish-brown. Contains gyromitrin — potentially FATAL.

Xanthoria parietina.notes: Common orange lichen. Bright orange lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Nectria cinnabarina.notes: Coral spot. Small red spots on dead wood. Inedible.

Sarcomyxa serotina.notes: Late oyster. Olive-yellow gills on wood. Edible.

Inonotus obliquus.notes: Chaga. Black burnt-looking growth on birch. Inedible as food.

Panellus stipticus.notes: Bitter oyster. Small bracket, bioluminescent. Inedible.

Hypogymnia physodes.notes: Puffed lichen. Grey-green foliose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Hygrophoropsis aurantiaca.notes: False chanterelle. Orange, thinner gills, grows on wood. Poisonous.

Cladonia rangiferina.notes: Reindeer lichen. Fruticose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Platismatia glauca.notes: Ruffled lichen. Grey-green foliose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Calycina citrina.notes: Lemon discos. Tiny yellow cups. Inedible.

Cladonia stellaris.notes: Star-tipped reindeer lichen. Fruticose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Amanita citrina.notes: False death cap. Yellowish cap, volva at base. Poisonous.

Gyromitra gigas.notes: Snow morel. Large brain-like cap. Poisonous.

Crucibulum laeve.notes: Common birds nest. Small cups with egg-like peridioles. Inedible.

Daedaleopsis tricolor.notes: Multicolor gill polypore. Bracket with gill-like pores. Inedible.

Stereum hirsutum.notes: Hairy curtain crust. Thin bracket with hairy upper side. Inedible.

Paxillus involutus.notes: Brown roll-rim. Inrolled cap margin. Poisonous — can cause immune reaction.

Lactarius turpis.notes: Ugly milkcap. Dark olive-brown cap. Poisonous.

Chlorociboria aeruginascens.notes: Green elfcup. Tiny turquoise cup fungus. Inedible.

Chondrostereum purpureum.notes: Silverleaf fungus. Violet crust on wood. Inedible.

Phaeophyscia orbicularis.notes: Shadow lichen. Grey foliose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Peltigera praetextata.notes: Scaly dog lichen. Foliose lichen. Lichen, not a mushroom.

Agaricus essettei.notes: Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume without positive identification by a certified mycologist.

Clitocybe nitrophila.notes: Nitrophilous clitocybe. Small greyish cap on nutrient-rich soils. Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume.

Infundibulicybe costata.notes: Ribbed funnel cap. Funnel-shaped cap with ribbed stem. Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume.

Mycena pelliculosa.notes: Small bonnet with a pelliculose cap surface. Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume.

Russula globispora.notes: Globose-spored russula. Brittle gilled mushroom. Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume.

Russula lepida.notes: Pretty russula. Pink to purple cap. Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume.

Russula mairei.notes: Maire's russula. Brittle gilled mushroom. Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume.

Russula nigricans.notes: Blackening brittlegill. Flesh bruises black. Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume.

Russula undulata.notes: Wavy russula. Undulating cap margin. Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume.

Russula velutipes.notes: Velvet-footed russula. Velvety stem surface. Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume.

Singerocybe phaeophthalma.notes: Pearly powdercap. Small grey-brown cap. Edibility unknown or unverified. Do not consume.
